DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SHABAN SHAKOORI, JULIA SHAKOORI'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF ALI SHAKOORI IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE DEPONENT ALI SHAKOORI SHALL APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION WITHIN ONE WEEK OF THIS ORDER, AND SHALL PRODUCE AT DEPOSITION THE DOCUMENTS IDENTIFIED IN THE ATTACHMENT (EXH. A) TO THE ORIGINAL DEPOSITION NOTICE SERVED FEBRUARY 6. BECAUSE THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER THE ORIGINAL DEPOSITION NOTICE WAS IN FACT DELIVERED (AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND HIS STAFF DENY THAT IT WAS), SANCTIONS ARE DENIED AS TO BOTH SIDES. THE PARTIES SHOULD HAVE WORKED TOGETHER TO MAKE THE DEPOSITION HAPPEN WITHOUT COURT INTERVENTION. ORDER IS SIGNED. MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-10-2019 IN 206, PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT ALI R. SHAKOORI'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM JURY WAIVER [PREVIOUSLY FILED JULY 23, 2019]. ROBERT JAKE KAHN, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MARC STIMPERT, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. HAVING CONSIDERED THE MOVING AND OPPOSING PAPERS IN THIS MATTER, AS WELL AS THE ARGUMENTS OF COUNSEL, THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT ALI SHAKOORI'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM JURY WAIVER. THE COURT HAS LOOKED AT CCP 631(F) AND (G) AND DAY V. ROSENTHAL (1985) 170 CA3D 1125,1176. EVEN THOUGH INADVERTENT MISTAKE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT ARE NOT PART OF THE STATUTE, THE COURT CONSIDERS INADVERTENCE UNDER CCP 631(G) AND UNDER WINSTON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 196 CAL.APP.3D 600, 602 AND ITS PART OF THE COURT'S DISCRETION. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE EXPLANATION FOR THE INADVERTENCE IS NOT PERSUASIVE TO THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Answer And Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew McClelland, Esq. 415-426-3521of Lunski & Stimpert LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court admonishes Counsel. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHABAN SHAKOORI, JULIA SHAKOORI's Motion To Amend Answer And Cross-Complaint.

Defendants and Cross-Complainants Shaban A. Shakoori and Julia Shakoori's motion to amend answer and cross-complaint is granted. California's strong policy favoring liberal amendment of pleadings supports the motion. Plaintiff Ali R. Shakoori has failed to show that the proposed amendments are barred by the sham pleading doctrine. "Critically, the sham pleading doctrine 'cannot be mechanically applied.' [Citation.] It 'is not intended to prevent honest complainants from correcting erroneous allegations or prevent the correction of ambiguous facts.'" (JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Ward (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 678, 690.) Further, the doctrine "was not intended to preclude plaintiffs from providing additional and noncontradictory allegations." (Leasequip, Inc. v. Dapeer (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 394, 405 n.6; see also Zakk v. Diesel (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 431 [new allegations regarding contract claims in third amended complaint "merely clarifies the basis for [plaintiff's] claims for relief" and sham pleading doctrine therefore did not apply].) Plaintiff may raise any challenge he has to the timeliness of the proposed new causes of action in the cross-complaint by demurrer or motion for summary judgment/adjudication. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Ali Shakoori
Off Calendar Mar-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert J. Kahn, Esq., Huguenin Kahn,LLP Telephoen 916-367-7098, appearing for Plaintiff;
Marc Stimpert, Esq,/Andrew McClelland Esq., of Lunski & Stimpert LLP. 415-426-3586 appearing for Defendant(s) Shaban A. Shakoori, et al;
Ken Brill, CSR #12797, Veritext, Telephone 415-274-9977

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHABAN SHAKOORI, JULIA SHAKOORI Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Ali Shakoori

Motion granted. The Court finds that the parties had agreed to February 22 as the deposition date for Mr. Shakoori. The notice served by mail by defendants' counsel on February 6 appears to have been properly and timely served. Why it was not received is a mystery, but under Evid. Code sec. 641, "A letter correctly addressed and properly mailed is presumed to have been received in the ordinary course of mail." The amended notice, which was electronically served on February 18, was not timely, but the deposition still should have gone forward as originally noticed on the agreed upon date. The Court orders that the deponent Ali Shakoori shall appear for deposition within one week of this order, and shall produce at deposition the documents identified in the attachment (Exh. A) to the original deposition notice served February 6. Because the Court cannot determine whether the original deposition notice was in fact delivered (and plaintiff's counsel and his staff deny that it was), sanctions are denied as to both sides. The parties should have worked together to make the deposition happen without court intervention.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: William Trupek; Court Reporter: Ken Brill, CSR #12797 (302/JPT)
